title: Dynamic Programming
summary: Solve complex problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems, storing results to avoid redundant computation.
overview: Dynamic programming (DP) is optimization through subproblem reuse. When a recursive solution computes the same subproblems repeatedly, DP caches results (memoization = top-down; tabulation = bottom-up). In frontend, DP thinking appears as memoization of expensive computations, useMemo dependencies, and avoiding redundant re-renders.
coreConcept: DP applies when: (1) optimal substructure — optimal solution contains optimal solutions to subproblems, and (2) overlapping subproblems — the same subproblems appear repeatedly. Memoization stores results in a map keyed by inputs. Tabulation fills a table iteratively from base cases forward.
signals: You can define the problem in terms of smaller same-structure problems

frontendScenarios.title: Memoizing Expensive Derived State
frontendScenarios.description: A component computes a filtered + sorted + grouped list from raw data. With useMemo and a correct dependency array, React avoids recomputation unless inputs change. This is top-down DP with React as the cache layer.

frontendScenarios.title: Text Diff / Edit Distance
frontendScenarios.description: Computing the diff between two strings (for a code editor or collaborative document) uses DP edit distance (Levenshtein). The DP table stores minimum edit cost to transform substring i..j.

dataThinking: DP problems have a state space (the set of distinct subproblems) and a transition function (how to compute a state from previous states). Identify what changes between subproblems — that's your state. In frontend, this maps to: what inputs determine the output? Cache on those inputs. React.memo, useMemo, and useCallback are all DP in disguise.

naiveApproach.description: Plain recursion without caching. Recomputes the same subproblems exponentially.

betterApproach.description: Add memoization (Map keyed by state) to recursive solution. Or convert to bottom-up tabulation for better space efficiency.
betterApproach.complexity: O(n²) or O(n*k) depending on problem; exponential becomes polynomial

visualizationSteps.label: Coin change: coins=[1,5,6], amount=11
visualizationSteps.explanation: We want minimum coins to make 11. dp[0]=0 (base case). Fill dp[1..11] bottom-up.

visualizationSteps.label: Fill dp[1..6]
visualizationSteps.explanation: dp[5]=1 (one 5-coin). dp[6]=1 (one 6-coin). dp[1..4] each need that many 1-coins.

practiceItems.title: Memoize Expensive Component Calculation
practiceItems.question: A dashboard computes 'total revenue by category' from a large transactions array. It's called in render. Refactor it to use memoization so it only recomputes when transactions change.
practiceItems.hint1: useMemo with [transactions] dependency array. The computation itself is a reduce over the array.
practiceItems.hint2: For a non-React version, implement a memoize() higher-order function that caches by stringified args.

practiceItems.title: Longest Common Subsequence
practiceItems.question: Given two strings, find the length of their longest common subsequence. This underlies diff algorithms used in version control and collaborative editing.
practiceItems.hint1: DP table of size (m+1) x (n+1). dp[i][j] = LCS length of s1[0..i-1] and s2[0..j-1].

reflectionPrompts: React.memo, useMemo, and useCallback are all forms of memoization. How do they relate to the DP concept of caching subproblem results?
What is the difference between top-down (memoization) and bottom-up (tabulation) DP? When would you prefer one over the other?
How do you identify the 'state' in a DP problem? What question do you ask?
Edit distance is O(m*n) time and space. How would you reduce the space to O(min(m,n))?
